title: Caraguata serrata
taxonomy: Caraguata serrata is classified in the family Malpighiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as Barbados cherry or acerola cherry family. The Malpighiaceae family is part of the order Malpighiales, which includes a wide range of plants with distinctive fruit and flower characteristics. Caraguata serrata is further classified into the genus Caraguata, which consists of a small number of species found in South America. Within the genus Caraguata, serrata is one of the recognized species, characterized by its unique serrated leaves and specific growth habits. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical features, as well as its phylogenetic relationships with other plant species.
